section: Chapter 8: Measurement of Time and Motion

questions.question: What is motion?
questions.correct_answer: Change in the position of an object with respect to time
questions.explanation: An object is said to be in motion if its position changes relative to a reference point.

questions.question: What is speed?
questions.explanation: Speed tells how fast an object is moving and is usually measured in m/s or km/h.

questions.question: What is the difference between uniform and non-uniform motion?
questions.correct_answer: Uniform motion: moves equal distance in equal time; Non-uniform motion: moves unequal distances in equal time
questions.explanation: Uniform motion has constant speed, non-uniform motion has changing speed.

questions.question: How is average speed calculated?
questions.correct_answer: Total distance traveled divided by total time taken
questions.explanation: Average speed = Total distance / Total time

questions.question: What is a reference point?
questions.correct_answer: A fixed point used to determine motion
questions.explanation: The position of an object is measured relative to a reference point.

questions.question: What is a stopwatch used for?
questions.explanation: Stopwatches are used in experiments, sports, and motion studies.

questions.question: Define instantaneous speed.
questions.correct_answer: Speed of an object at a particular moment
questions.explanation: It is the speed read at a specific instant rather than over a time interval.

questions.question: What is a motion graph?
questions.correct_answer: A graph showing the relation between distance and time or speed and time
questions.explanation: Motion graphs help visualize how objects move over time.

questions.question: What is the SI unit of time?
questions.explanation: Time is measured in seconds, minutes, hours, etc., with second as the standard unit.

questions.question: Give an example of uniform motion.
questions.correct_answer: A car moving at a constant speed of 60 km/h on a straight road
questions.explanation: It covers equal distances in equal intervals of time.

questions.question: Give an example of non-uniform motion.
questions.correct_answer: A bus stopping at traffic signals on a city road
questions.explanation: Its speed changes due to stops and starts.

questions.question: Why is measurement of time important in studying motion?
questions.correct_answer: Because speed and other motion parameters are calculated using time
questions.explanation: Without time, it is impossible to quantify motion accurately.

notes.title: Motion
notes.content: Change in position relative to a reference point; measured using distance and time.

notes.title: Speed
notes.content: Distance traveled per unit time; uniform or non-uniform.

notes.title: Time Measurement
notes.content: Use of clocks and stopwatches for accurate measurement of intervals.

notes.title: Motion Graphs
notes.content: Graphs showing distance-time or speed-time relationships.

facts.fact: SI unit of time is second (s).
Uniform motion covers equal distances in equal intervals of time.
Non-uniform motion has changing speed over equal intervals of time.
Instantaneous speed is the speed at a particular instant.
